title: EO Optical Payload Sub-Assembly
entry_mechanism: EO payload manufacturer establishing Saudi operations under PSAP with KACST optical research partnership
executive_summary:
Earth observation optical payloads — the telescope, focal plane array, and signal processing electronics that capture satellite imagery — are among the most technically demanding and commercially valuable components in the small satellite value chain. Saudi Arabia's aspiration to operate a sovereign EO constellation (for national development monitoring, border surveillance, and commercial imagery sales) requires eventual Saudi EO payload capability.

Node SS-6 targets Phase I establishment of sub-assembly capability for EO optical payloads: Saudi technicians assembling and integrating optical sub-components (secondary mirrors, focal plane arrays, electronic interfaces) under the technical supervision of an international EO payload manufacturer, with the goal of progressive Saudi engineering content growth leading to independent payload design in Phase III.

Saudi Arabia's geography — vast territories with consistent, cloud-free atmospheric conditions — makes EO validation testing uniquely advantageous. A Saudi EO payload assembly facility would have the world's best natural test environment immediately adjacent.

companies.name: Airbus Defence and Space (EO Payloads)
companies.rationale: Airbus designs and manufactures EO payloads for multiple programmes. Saudi payload assembly under Airbus supervision with progressive technology transfer.
companies.contact: Airbus Defence VP EO Payload, International

companies.name: Thales Alenia Space (EO)
companies.rationale: Thales Alenia has EO payload design and manufacturing and Saudi Arabia government relationships.

companies.name: SSTL (EO missions)
companies.rationale: SSTL designs EO payloads for small satellites and has Saudi Arabia engagement.

companies.name: Spacety (China — depends on policy)
companies.rationale: Chinese EO payload manufacturers offer competitive pricing and active international partnerships. Policy and technology dependency considerations apply.

companies.name: Leonardo (EO Division)
companies.rationale: Leonardo has EO instrument manufacturing and active Gulf relationships.

partners.name: KACST Optical Technology Research
partners.current: Optical engineering research; some telescope design; remote sensing research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST EO payload research programme; focal plane array characterisation; Saudi optical engineer pipeline; KACST as Phase I test facility for Saudi EO sub-assemblies
partners.timeline: 36 months to Saudi EO sub-assembly capability

partners.name: SSA / Riyadh Smart Parcel
partners.upgrade: EO payload clean room assembly facility; Airbus or SSTL supervised assembly programme; 15 Saudi optical engineers

partners.name: KAUST Applied Physics
partners.current: Photonics research; optical materials; imaging systems
partners.upgrade: Space-grade optical system research; focal plane array performance research; KAUST-KACST joint EO payload research

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Geosciences
partners.current: High-resolution EO data user; some optical systems for industrial inspection
partners.upgrade: Aramco-funded Saudi EO payload development; specific spectral requirements for oil field monitoring integrated into Saudi EO payload design
